blurb:
This is the real deal French bakery that makes everything from scratch daily. The owner is French and you can tell the difference in quality. It's a bright, cheerful spot perfect for weekend brunch with friends or grabbing pastries and coffee on the way to work.

The croissants are flaky and buttery like they should be, and the quiche changes daily but is always excellent. I love their baguettes — they're what every other bakery wishes they could make. The petit fours are beautiful and actually taste as good as they look, perfect for bringing to a party.

Get there early on weekends because the good stuff sells out fast. They close early on Sundays and are usually picked clean by then, so don't wait if you want the full selection.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
This place has ruined regular pizza for me. It's in the Blue Stone shopping center, and while it looks like just another strip mall spot from the outside, the pizza here is genuinely extraordinary. The crust is thin and crispy, and you can tell they use high-quality ingredients. Good for date night or family dinner when you want something special but not stuffy.

The Margherita is perfect if you want to taste what real pizza should be like. My kids love the Pepperoni, and even my picky eater cleans his plate. They also do great salads if someone in your group isn't feeling pizza. Everything comes out fast even when they're busy.

The only downside is it can get loud when packed, but that's because everyone's having a good time. Definitely make a reservation on weekends. They're closed between lunch and dinner some days, so check before you go.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This little spot in a strip mall off Franklin Gateway has become my go-to when I want fresh, healthy food that doesn't cost a fortune. The owner is usually there and genuinely cares about making good food. It's casual with a few tables, perfect for a quick lunch or grabbing dinner to take home.

The shawarma is what I always order — they slice the meat fresh and pile it high with all the fixings. My wife loves the falafel, which is crispy outside and fluffy inside, nothing like the dry hockey pucks you get some places. The hummus and pita are made fresh daily and worth getting as a side.

Great option when you want something different from the usual chain restaurants, and the prices make it easy to feed the whole family without spending a ton.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is where we go for anniversaries and special occasions when we want to feel fancy without driving downtown. The restaurant sits right on the Chattahoochee River, and if you can snag a window table, the view is beautiful, especially at sunset. It's definitely a splurge, but worth it for date nights or celebrating something important.

The seafood is what they do best. I always get the salmon, and my husband loves their crab cakes. The portions are generous for fine dining, and everything comes out beautifully presented. The service is attentive without being stuffy, which I appreciate.

Make a reservation, especially for weekend dinners. The parking can be tricky during busy times, but they do have valet if you don't mind paying extra. Sunday brunch is also popular if you want to try the place without the full dinner price tag.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This food hall is perfect when your family can't agree on what to eat. My kids want pizza, my wife wants Thai, I want barbecue — problem solved. It's right on the square, so you can walk around downtown after eating or catch an event at the pavilion.

With twenty different vendors, there's legitimately something for everyone. The barbecue spot is solid, and the Thai place makes a good pad thai. I usually end up getting something different each time I go, which keeps it interesting.

Great for lunch meetings, family dinners when everyone wants different things, or grabbing food before a concert on the square. Parking can be tricky on busy weekends, but it's worth circling the block a few times.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is the place we end up at 2am when nothing else is open or when my mother-in-law visits and wants "good old-fashioned American food." It's been a Marietta institution forever, with that classic neon sign you can see from the highway. The atmosphere is pure diner — booths, coffee that keeps coming, and servers who've probably been there longer than you've lived in town.

The menu is absolutely massive, which can be overwhelming, but stick to the breakfast classics and you can't go wrong. Their pancakes are huge and fluffy, and the Greek specialties like gyros are surprisingly good. I always get the breakfast platter with eggs, bacon, and hash browns — it's exactly what you want from a diner.

Being open 24/7 makes it perfect for late-night cravings, early morning meetings before work, or when you need to feed a crowd without breaking the bank.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat, this is a great place to try it without the downtown Atlanta prices. They bring different cuts of meat to your table and slice them fresh — it's fun and the kids love the experience. The atmosphere is relaxed and family-friendly, good for celebrating something special or date night.

The cheese bread they bring out first is addictive, so pace yourself. The picanha and lamb are standouts from the meat selection. They also have a solid salad bar if you want to balance out all that protein, plus the feijoada stew is authentic and hearty.

Just know you'll leave very full and probably need a nap. It's not cheap for a family, but the experience and quality make it worth it for special occasions.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
My wife and I stumbled on this place last year and now we're regulars. It's in Sandy Springs Place, and the vibe inside is fun and energetic without being too loud. The service is always on point, and you can tell they actually care about the food they're putting out. Great for a casual date night or dinner with friends.

Start with the Esquite if you've never had it before. It's Mexican street corn and absolutely addictive. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher is refreshing and perfect for sharing. The tacos are obviously the star, and they're all good, but I keep going back to the same ones every time because they're that consistent.

They're open late most nights, which is rare around here and super convenient when you want good food after 10pm. The place gets busy on weekends, but the wait is never too bad and worth it.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is hands down one of the best dinner spots in Sandy Springs. I went here for girls' night based on a friend's recommendation and now I can't stop talking about it. The atmosphere is warm and sophisticated, perfect for date night or celebrating with friends. The service is exceptional and they clearly know what they're doing.

Start with the hummus and definitely try the wings if you're feeling adventurous. The chicken shish kabobs are incredible, and the rice with lentils and raisins is unlike anything I've had before. The cocktails are all well-made, and save room for the tiramisu because it's worth every calorie. Everything we ordered was perfectly executed.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to get seated. Make a reservation for dinner because word is getting out about this place and it's getting harder to get a table.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is my go-to when I'm craving authentic Indian food but don't want to drive all the way into town. It's in the Perimeter Center area, nothing fancy inside, but the food is consistently good and the portions are generous. Perfect for feeding a family without breaking the bank.

The biryani is what they're known for, and it's legit. The chicken biryani comes with tender meat and perfectly spiced rice that actually tastes like it was made by someone's grandmother. They serve everything family-style, so it's great for sharing or having leftovers for lunch the next day.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner some days, so check the hours if you're going mid-afternoon. The service can be slow when they're busy, but the food is worth the wait.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA
